mission.title: coredns: Reverse zones should be calculated for the full CIDR range
mission.description: Reverse zones should be calculated for the full CIDR range. This issue affects 2+ users.

steps.title: Identify coredns troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your coredns deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Reverse zones should be calculated for the full CIDR range

steps.title: Review coredns configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns
```
When specifying a reverse DNS zone using CIDR notation the full range of zones are not calculated.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Reverse zones should be calculated for the full CIDR range
steps.description:
### 1. Why is this pull request needed and what does it do?
This pull request calculates the full reverse zones for a given CIDR block. It is based on https://github.com/ynikanchyk/coredns/pull/2 although there were some small changes that needed to be updated to get all tests passing.

### 2. Which issues (if any) are related?
https://github.com/coredns/coredns/issues/2779
https://github.com/ynikanchyk/coredns/pull/2

### 3. Which documentation changes (if any) need to be made?
Possibly will

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Reverse zones should be calculated for the full is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns -n coredns --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.
